title: Flood Risk Assessment in Northern Thailand
description: Developed SWAT hydrological models to evaluate flood risk and water availability in Northern Thai watersheds under climate stressors.
shortDescription: SWAT-based flood and water-security modelling for Northern Thailand
content:
# Flood Risk Assessment Project

This project evaluates flood hazard behavior and watershed response under changing climate and land-use conditions in Northern Thailand.

objectives: Develop SWAT models for 5 key watersheds
Assess flood risk under different climate scenarios
Identify nature-based solutions for risk reduction

title: MESSHI: Suspended Sediment Monitoring
description: Established a suspended-sediment monitoring network in Himalayan rivers using OBS sensors, field sampling, and citizen-science workflows.
shortDescription: Himalayan sediment monitoring with citizen science and sensor networks
content:
# MESSHI Project Overview

Built a field-to-lab workflow for sediment monitoring in remote Himalayan watersheds with strong community engagement.

objectives: Deploy OBS sensors at 10+ remote sites

title: CWIS Geospatial Sanitation Mapping
description: Preparing and integrating geospatial sanitation infrastructure datasets for citywide inclusive sanitation planning in Asian cities.
shortDescription: GIS support for Citywide Inclusive Sanitation planning
content:
# CWIS Geospatial Sanitation Mapping

Supporting evidence-based sanitation planning through city-scale geospatial integration and visualization.

objectives: Map service coverage and infrastructure gaps
Create decision-ready map outputs for stakeholders
